title: scabiosa communis subsp. sylvatica
intro: Scabiosa communis subsp. sylvatica, also known as woodland pincushion, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the genus Scabiosa, characterized by its delicate blue or pink flowers and soft, velvety leaves. Traditionally, Scabiosa communis subsp. sylvatica has been used in herbal medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe wounds and reduce inflammation. The medicinal properties of this plant have also been associated with various health benefits, including its potential to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and promote wound healing. Scabiosa communis subsp. sylvatica is often prepared in herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, although its use should be approached with caution and under the guidance of a qualified healthcare professional.
